<!-- unit 1 -->
# Section Heading

Some introductory paragraph that explains a fictional concept and sets up
a scenario where the user is interested in learning about Rust streaming
parsers built on top of pulldown-cmark and integrated with a TUI surface.

## Subsection

Here is a list of things to consider when building one:

* Boundary detection between blocks.
* Lazy continuation of paragraphs that span multiple lines.
* Setext headings that are realised only once `===` arrives.
* Fenced code blocks whose closing fence has not yet streamed in.
* Tables with header alignment markers in the second row.

> A blockquote with **strong** and *emphasised* prose, including
> `inline code` and a [link](https://example.com/streaming-md).
> Lazy continuation should keep this in the same blockquote even though
> the source wraps across multiple lines.

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut inc = IncrementalParser::new();
    for chunk in chunks {
        inc.push(chunk);
    }
    println!("{:?}", inc.blocks());
}
```

| Header A | Header B | Header C |
|----------|----------|----------|
| cell 1a  | cell 1b  | cell 1c  |
| cell 2a  | cell 2b  | cell 2c  |
| cell 3a  | cell 3b  | cell 3c  |

---

1. First numbered item with some prose.
2. Second item that includes `inline code`.
3. Third item linking to <https://example.com>.

Plain paragraph at the end of the unit. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet,
consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore
et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud
exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat.

<!-- unit 18 -->
# Section Heading

Some introductory paragraph that explains a fictional concept and sets up
a scenario where the user is interested in learning about Rust streaming
parsers built on top of pulldown-cmark and integrated with a TUI surface.

## Subsection

Here is a list of things to consider when building one:

* Boundary detection between blocks.
* Lazy continuation of paragraphs that span multiple lines.
* Setext headings that are realised only once `===` arrives.
* Fenced code blocks whose closing fence has not yet streamed in.
* Tables with header alignment markers in the second row.

> A blockquote with **strong** and *emphasised* prose, including
> `inline code` and a [link](https://example.com/streaming-md).
> Lazy continuation should keep this in the same blockquote even though
> the source wraps across multiple lines.

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut inc = IncrementalParser::new();
    for chunk in chunks {
        inc.push(chunk);
    }
    println!("{:?}", inc.blocks());
}
```
